steps.title: Identify what would support the claim
steps.content: The underlined claim asserts that 'Aha Pūnana Leo caused an increase in young people speaking Hawaiian. To directly support this claim, we need evidence showing the number of young Hawaiian speakers increased.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the claim
steps.content: Choice A provides exactly this: fewer than 50 children could speak Hawaiian initially, but now over 2,000 'Aha Pūnana Leo students speak it. Choices B, C, and D address unrelated topics—population statistics, linguistic similarities, and other ethnic communities—without showing an increase in Hawaiian speakers. That matches choice A — A is correct.
